name: Cloudinary
description: Cloud media management — upload images and video, apply transformations (resize, transcode, trim, crop, extract frame, audio extract, concat), and deliver via CDN. Account-scoped: every account has its own cloud_name that becomes part of the URL path.

credential_fields.name: cloud_name
credential_fields.label: Cloud Name
credential_fields.description: Your Cloudinary account's cloud name — visible at the top of https://console.cloudinary.com. Used as a URL path segment, not a credential.

credential_fields.name: api_secret
credential_fields.label: API Secret
credential_fields.description: Paired with API Key for HTTP Basic auth.

tools.name: upload
tools.description: Upload bytes or a URL to Cloudinary. Accepts a remote URL, a data URI, or an S3/GCS-style path. Returns the asset's public_id, secure_url, format, dimensions. Use this for ingesting source media before applying transformations via `explicit`.

resource_type.description: Path segment. `auto` lets Cloudinary detect from the file. Required because the path interpolates this value.

file.description: Source: HTTPS URL, data: URI, or S3 URL (s3://bucket/key) when account has S3 fetch enabled.

public_id.description: Stable identifier the asset will be addressable by. Optional; auto-generated when omitted.

folder.description: Asset folder (e.g. "agents/inbox"). Distinct from public_id; the folder is metadata, the public_id is the address.

eager.description: Pipe-separated transformation chain to apply eagerly (e.g. "c_fill,w_300,h_200"). Useful for pre-baking thumbnails.

context.description: Pipe-separated key=value pairs for contextual metadata.

upload_preset.description: Pre-configured preset name. With Basic auth + API secret you can omit this; required only for unsigned uploads.

tools.name: explicit
tools.description: Apply transformations to an already-uploaded asset and (optionally) eagerly produce derived versions. Use this for post-upload trim/resize/transcode/crop/concat. The `eager` parameter is the heavy hitter: pass an array of transformation strings and Cloudinary produces each derivative + returns the URLs in the response.

resource_type.description: Path segment. Use `image` for stills, `video` for clips/audio.

public_id.description: Identifier of the asset to transform.

eager.description:
Pipe-separated list of transformation strings, each one is `<param>,<param>,...`. Cheat-sheet for the 7 ffmpeg-equivalents:
  - trim:           so_<startSec>,du_<durationSec>
  - resize:         c_scale,w_<W>,h_<H>          (or c_limit / c_fill)
  - transcode:      f_<format>                    (e.g. f_mp4, f_webm)
  - crop:           c_crop,w_<W>,h_<H>,x_<X>,y_<Y>
  - extract_frame:  so_<sec>,f_jpg                 (image:explicit on a video)
  - audio_extract:  af_<format>                    (e.g. af_mp3) + f_<format>
  - concat:         fl_splice,l_video:<other_id>   (overlay-style splice)
Multiple transformation chains: pipe-separate them.

eager_async.description: When true, derivatives are produced async and you'll be notified via eager_notification_url. Default false (sync, response includes finished URLs).

invalidate.description: Invalidate CDN cache for the asset + its derivatives.

tools.name: destroy
tools.description: Delete an uploaded asset and its CDN cache. Permanent.

invalidate.description: Invalidate CDN cache. Recommended when re-uploading under the same public_id.

tools.name: list_resources
tools.description: Admin API: list assets in this account, optionally filtered by prefix / tag / context. Returns metadata (no bytes) for browsing or sync.

prefix.description: Filter to public_ids starting with this prefix. Cheap way to scope to a folder.

tags.description: Include tags in the response. Default false.
